structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Start/Stop Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Supply fan of dual-fan dual-duct heating VAV AHU
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The supply fan runs during Warmup and Setback modes, and during Occupied mode while there are any heating-fan requests, with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: System in Warmup Mode, Setback Mode, or Occupied Mode with heating-fan requests.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan runs; minimum runtime 15 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan shall run when system is in the Warmup Mode  and Setback Mode , and during Occupied Mode  while there are any heating -fan requests with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.

evidence_quote: Fan shall run when system is in the Warmup Mode  and Setback Mode , and during Occupied Mode  while there are any heating -fan requests with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.
evidence_text: Fan shall run when system is in the Warmup Mode  and Setback Mode , and during Occupied Mode  while there are any heating -fan requests with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Totalize VAV Airflow for Diagnostics
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Sum VAV box airflow rates to obtain overall supply air rate without AFMS, used for display, diagnostics, and filter alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Software point totalizes airflow from VAV boxes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point. VAV box airflow rates are summed to obtain overall supply air rate without the need for an AFMS at the air -handler discharge.  This is used only for display and diagnostics and filter alarm.

evidence_quote: Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point. VAV box airflow rates are summed to obtain overall supply air rate without the need for an AFMS at the air -handler discharge.  This is used only for display and diagnostics and filter alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Static Pressure Control via Supply Fan Speed
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Supply fan speed is controlled to maintain duct static pressure at setpoint when fan is proven ON. For small Zone Groups, provide multiple gain sets based on load indicator.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan speed modulates to maintain DSP at setpoint. Gains may vary based on load.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Supply fan speed is controlled to maintain DSP at setpoint when the fan is proven ON. Where the Zone Group s served by the system are small, provide multiple sets of gains that are used in the control loop as a function of a load indicator (such as supply -fan airflow rate, the area of the Zone Group s that are occupied, etc.).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Filter Pressure Drop Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of alarm limit or 12.5 Pa for 10 minutes when airflow exceeds 20%: Level 4. Alarm limit varies with total airflow as DPx = DP100 * x^1.4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Filter pressure drop > max(alarm limit, 12.5 Pa) for 10 minutes when airflow > 20% of design.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm.
configurable_values: DP100: high-limit pressure drop at design airflow from filter manufacturer
Constant threshold: minimum accuracy of transducer and A/D converter combination
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: DP > max(alarm limit, 12.5 Pa) for 10 min and airflow > 20%
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit  or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when airflow (expressed as a percentage of design airflow or design  speed if total airflow is not known) exceeds 20% : Level 4.  The alarm limit shall vary with total airflow (if available; use fan speed if total airflow is not known) as follows:
𝐷𝑃𝑥=𝐷𝑃100(𝑥)1.4

evidence_quote:

𝐷𝑃𝑥=𝐷𝑃100(𝑥)1.4

structured_payload_candidate.title: Required Points for DFDD Heating AHU AFDD
structured_payload_candidate.summary: AFDD requires SAT, RAT, DSP, SATSP, DSPSP, HC, FS points available.
configurable_values: HC: heating coil valve position command (0% to 100%)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each AHU:
a. SAT = supply air temperature
b. RAT = return air temperature
c. DSP = duct static pressure
d. SATSP = supply air temperature setpoint
e. DSPSP = duct static pressure setpoint
f. HC = heating coil valve position command; 0%  ≤ HC ≤ 100%
g. FS = fan speed command; 0%  ≤ FS ≤ 100%

structured_payload_candidate.title: Rolling Averages for AFDD
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling time are calculated for SAT, RAT, DSP. Operator adjustable averaging window and sampling period.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate rolling averages continuously.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Five-minute rolling averages with 1 -minute sampling time of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently
1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature
2. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature
3. DSP avg = rolling average of duct static pressure

evidence_quote:
Five-minute rolling averages with 1 -minute sampling time of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently
1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature
2. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature
3. DSP avg = rolling average of duct static pressure

structured_payload_candidate.title: DFDD Heating AHU AFDD Internal Variables
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Internal variables with default values: ΔTSF=1°C, εSAT=1°C, εRAT=1°C, εVFDSPD=5%, εDSP=25 Pa, ModeDelay=30 min, AlarmDelay=30 min, TestModeDelay=120 min.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Table 5.17.4.4 DFDD Heating AHU AFDD Internal Variables
Variable Name  Description  Default Value
∆TSF Temperature rise across supply fan  1°C (2° F)
εSAT Temperature error threshold for SAT sensor  1°C (2° F)
εRAT Temperature error threshold for RAT sensor  1°C (2° F)
εVFDSPD  VFD speed error threshold  5%
εDSP Duct static pressure error threshold  25 Pa (0.1”)
ModeDelay  Time in minutes to suspend Fault Condition evaluation after a change in Mode  30
AlarmDelay  Time in minutes that a Fault Condition must persist before triggering an alarm  30
TestModeDelay  Time in minutes that Test Mode is enabled  120

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fault Condition 1: Duct Static Pressure Too Low at Full Fan Speed
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: AHU duct static pressure and fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If DSP < DSPSP - εDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - εVFDSPD, then fault: duct static pressure too low with fan at full speed.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: DSP < DSPSP - εDSP AND VFDSPD ≥ 99% - εVFDSPD
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses: VFD problem, mechanical problem with fan, fan undersized, SAT setpoint too high.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
FC#1  Equation  DSP < DSPSP - ƐDSP
and
VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD
Description  Duct static pressure is too low with fan at full speed
Possible
Diagnosis  Problem with VFD
Mechanical problem with fan
Fan undersized
SAT Setpoint too high (too much zone demand)

HW temperature too low or HW unavailable
Gas or electric heat unavailable

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fault Condition 3: Temperature Rise Across Inactive Heating Coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If RAT_avg - SAT_avg ≥ sqrt(εSAT²+εRAT²) + ΔTSF and HC = 0%, then fault: temperature rise across inactive heating coil.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses: HC sensor error, valve stuck open/leaking, gas/electric heat stuck on.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
FC#3  Equation  RAT AVG - SAT AVG ≥  √ƐSAT2+ ƐRAT2 + ∆TSF
and
HC = 0 %
Description  Temperature rise across inactive heating coil
Possible
Diagnosis  HCET sensor error
HCLT  sensor error
Heating coil valve stuck open or leaking
Gas or electric heat stuck on

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fault Condition Evaluation Suspension
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions is suspended when AHU is not operating, and for ModeDelay minutes after a change in mode of any Zone Group served.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: AHU not operating; or within ModeDelay minutes after mode change of any served Zone Group.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suspend evaluation.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When AHU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to
Occupied Mode ) of any Zone Group  served by the AHU

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Test Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Test mode temporarily sets ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of AFDD, then normal fault detection resumes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.
